title: 'Verify call edges are real: test TS and LSP edge sources independently'
---
## What\nThe `lsp_call_edges` table has a `source` column (`treesitter` or `lsp`). Currently:\n- Tree-sitter edges are generated by `generate_ts_call_edges` which uses regex-like call name extraction and symbol resolution. The unit tests in `ts_callgraph.rs` test these functions in isolation.\n- LSP edges would come from `callHierarchy/outgoingCalls` via `collect_call_edges` in `lsp_communication.rs`. But nobody calls this in production.\n- The integration test `test_callgraph_and_blastradius_on_real_repo` runs the TS pipeline but doesn't verify edge accuracy against known code.\n\nWe need tests that:\n1. Given known Rust source (`fn foo() { bar(); }`), verify TS call edges contain `foo → bar`\n2. Given the same source, verify LSP call edges (via real rust-analyzer) contain `foo → bar`\n3. Verify the `source` column correctly distinguishes TS vs LSP edges\n4. Verify `write_ts_edges` preserves existing LSP edges (test exists but worth verifying in integration)\n\nFiles:\n- `swissarmyhammer-code-context/tests/integration_test.rs`\n- `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ts_callgraph.rs` (existing tests may need strengthening)\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Integration test with known call graph verifies exact TS edges\n- [ ] Integration test with real rust-analyzer verifies exact LSP call edges\n- [ ] Both tests use the same source code for direct comparison\n- [ ] Edge `source` column is verified in both cases\n\n## Tests\n- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-code-context -- test_ts_call_edges_known_graph` passes\n- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-code-context -- test_lsp_call_edges_known_graph --ignored` passes with rust-analyzer"
